text:
A person holding an airman certificate shall keep the certificate in the person’s possession when the person is operating an aircraft within this state or serving in connection with an aircraft flown or operated in this state. The person shall present the certificate for inspection on the demand of:

(1) a passenger;

(2) a peace officer of this state; or

(3) an official, manager, or person in charge of an airport or landing field in this state on which the person lands an aircraft or performs a service.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 3
### Chapter 24. Operation of Aircraft
##### § 24.004 — Inspection of Airman Certificate

A person holding an airman certificate shall keep the certificate in the person’s possession when the person is operating an aircraft within this state or serving in connection with an aircraft flown or operated in this state. The person shall present the certificate for inspection on the demand of:

    (1) a passenger;

    (2) a peace officer of this state; or

    (3) an official, manager, or person in charge of an airport or landing field in this state on which the person lands an aircraft or performs a service.

*Acts 1995, 74th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 1995.*

subsections.text: A person holding an airman certificate shall keep the certificate in the person’s possession when the person is operating an aircraft within this state or serving in connection with an aircraft flown or operated in this state. The person shall present the certificate for inspection on the demand of:
a peace officer of this state; or
an official, manager, or person in charge of an airport or landing field in this state on which the person lands an aircraft or performs a service.

history.raw: Acts 1995, 74th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 1995.
